Details:
One study suggests that ultraviolet irradiation in presense of hydrogen peroxide does not degrade disulfoton effectively. Only 14 percent of disulfoton is degraded by an ultraviolet irradiation dose 200 mWsec/cm2 and hydrogen peroxide dose of 25 mg/l.
100% degradation can be achieved in 30 minutes at very high doses (340 mg/L) of hydrogen peroxide in acidic medium (pH = 2.8). These conditions are not typical of drinking water treatment.
